title: VS Code : "Remote DEV to podman" errors and "Attach to running container" not working.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**BUG REPORT **

/Access - unable to use vs code with remote podman RHEL platform

/Remote-Containers: Attach to running Container
/Remote-Container: Add Development Container Configuration Files

**Description**
VSC not work with remote RHEL 9 / podman server.
“Remote-Containers: Attach to running Container” 
“Remote-Container: Add Development Container Configuration Files”

<!--
Problem 1:
“Remote-Containers: Attach to running Container” 
 Wants to install Docker-Desktop on my Windows 10 desktop.  After install of Docket Desktop. Would not work.

Problem 2:
“Remote-Container: Add Development Container Configuration Files”
Roles thru entire process and at the end fails.
mkdir permissions denied.  
See attached docs 
error log
Outline of problem(s)

-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.LOCAL ENV:
   Windows 10 pro Desktop
   VSC: 1.71.2

2.REMOTE ENV:
   RHEL 9
   PODMAN 4.1.1
   podman-composer version  1.0.3
   Python39
   PIP3
   Alias docker=podman
   Alias docker-compose=podman-compose

   I have 3 containers created and running fine. These were created on Linux cli. (1) nginx,  nextc;loud, rhel 9 with python.

2 user accounts tested with:
   root user
   dev_user  with sudo 

3. Problem 2: 
 ctl+shift+P   select “Remote-Container: Add Development Container Configuration Files” 

**Describe the results you received:**
[2022-09-29T16:36:24.044Z] mkdir: cannot create directory ‘/root’: Permission denied
[2022-09-29T16:36:24.045Z] Exit code 1

[2022-09-29T16:36:24.045Z] Stop (10 ms): Run in container: mkdir -p '/root/.vscode-server/bin' && ln -snf '/vscode/vscode-server/bin/linux-x64/74b1f979648cc44d385a2286793c226e611f59e7' '/root/.vscode-server/bin/74b1f979648cc44d385a2286793c226e611f59e7'

[2022-09-29T16:36:24.049Z] Command in container failed: mkdir -p '/root/.vscode-server/bin' && ln -snf '/vscode/vscode-server/bin/linux-x64/74b1f979648cc44d385a2286793c226e611f59e7' '/root/.vscode-server/bin/74b1f979648cc44d385a2286793c226e611f59e7'

[2022-09-29T16:36:24.049Z] mkdir: cannot create directory ‘/root’: Permission denied
[2022-09-29T16:36:24.049Z] Exit code 1


**Describe the results you expected:**
for it to complete and create a dev container with python39 installed

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
devcontainer.json  was tested with 3 different configurations:

config 1:
	"remoteUser": "vscode"

config 2:
       //  "remoteUser": "vscode"
        "workspaceMount": "source=${localWorkspaceFolder},target=/workspace,type=bind,Z",
        "workspaceFolder": "/workspace",

        "runArgs": ["--userns=keep-id"],
        "containerUser": "vscode"

config 3:
        "remoteUser": "vscode"
        "workspaceMount": "source=${localWorkspaceFolder},target=/workspace,type=bind,Z",
        "workspaceFolder": "/workspace",

        "runArgs": ["--userns=keep-id"],
        "containerUser": "vscode"

config 4:
        //  "remoteUser": "vscode"
       
        "runArgs": ["--userns=keep-id"],
        "containerUser": "vscode"

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
podman version 4.1.1

**Output of `podman info`:**
```
(paste your output here)
```

Please see attachments.
[remoteContainers-2022-09-29T16-35-27.362Z.docx](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/9677758/remoteContainers-2022-09-29T16-35-27.362Z.docx)

comments.body: Any chance you can provide logs from the Podman API service?
Were you volume mounting in /root or is this failing inside of the container?

It does not get that far. It's during the creation process. You can see the
mkdir error permission denied in the report I sent you. Dud u read
attachments sent also?
John

On Thu, Sep 29, 2022, 1:29 PM Daniel J Walsh ***@***.***>
wrote:

> Were you volume mounting in /root or is this failing inside of the
> container?
>
> —

> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16000#issuecomment-1262662770>,

> .

> ***@***.***>
>

There are 2 issues.
1. cannot create podman dev container on my linux server using vsc.

2: cannot attach to running podman container using vsc.

That's basically it.
John

Not sure how? Can you provide command(s)?
Thank you

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I don't think this is a podman issue, you are using podman-compose which is not part of Podman, please open issue there.
